title: The Usher's Charge.
content:
Now, Jurymen, hear my advice 
    All kinds of vulgar prejudice
    I pray you set aside:
    With stern judicial frame of mind,
    From bias free of every kind,
    This trial must be tried!

    Oh, listen to the plaintiff's case:
    Observe the features of her face 
    The broken-hearted bride!
    Condole with her distress of mind:
    From bias free of every kind,
    This trial must be tried!

    And when amid the plaintiff's shrieks,
    The ruffianly defendant speaks 
    Upon the other side;
    What he may say you needn't mind 
    From bias free of every kind,
    This trial must be tried!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Schwenck Gilbert delivers a powerful performance in "The Usher's Charge."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
